findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Instructions execute unverified remote installer via curl-to-bash
findings.description: The skill directs the agent/user to download and immediately execute a shell script from an external domain using `curl -fsSL ... | bash`. This delegates trust to remote content at runtime and creates a supply-chain/code-execution risk if the endpoint, mirror, or delivery path is compromised. The same unsafe pattern is repeated in the CI example, increasing blast radius beyond local use.
findings.remediation: Do not pipe network content directly to a shell. Replace with a pinned, versioned installation method and verify checksums/signatures before execution. For CI, use trusted package registries or vendored artifacts instead of fetching and executing an unverified remote script.
